definition: affairs are important facts, events and activities relating to the government, economy, etc of a particular place
example: he’s an expert in foreign affairs (political/economic events in other countries)

definition: a celebrity is a famous person in entertainment or sport
example: he became an international celebrity

definition: justice is the treatment of people in a fair and morally correct way
example: Washington has long been regarded as a national leader in criminal justice

definition: if you accuse someone of doing something, you say that they have done something bad or illegal
example: opposition leaders regularly accuse the government of failure
she made a serious accusation against her boss
she made a serious accusation against her boss

definition: if you admit something, you say that you have done something wrong or something that people will not approve of
example: they’ll never openly admit being wrong
an admission of guilt

definition: if someone has an affair, they develop a sexual relationship with someone who is not their long-term partner
example: my husband had an affair 20 years ago

definition: an agreement is an official arrangement in which two or more people, organisations, or countries decide what should happen
example: despite the peace agreement, fighting continued for another week

definition: if you announce something, you officially make a statement about it, or tell people something that you think is very important
example: she’s just announced they’re splitting up
they decided not to make an official announcement

definition: if a company is bankrupt, it does not have enough money to pay all its debts and so cannot continue to exist. People who are bankrupt cannot pay their debts
example: a lot of companies have gone bankrupt recently
just one more major accident will bankrupt the company
the firm later fell into bankruptcy
just one more major accident will bankrupt the company

definition: if there is a ceasefire, the two sides in a war agree to stop fighting for a period of time
example: the two sides have agreed a ceasefire

definition: a deputy is someone who does their boss’s job when their boss is not present
example: the mayor and deputy mayor are elected annually by councillors
collocations: a deputy mayor/director/chairman etc Collocates:  deputise for someone

definition: an election is the process in which people vote to choose someone who will hold an official position. If someone is elected, they get enough votes to be chosen
example: he was elected to parliament in 2014
the economy was the most important factor in the election campaign
there’ll probably be a change of government after the general election (an election when everyone in the country can vote to choose the national government) \ the council has 36 elected members

definition: to expand means to increase in size. If a business expands, it starts to produce a bigger range of goods or opens new factories, shops, offices etc
example: the company expanded, and now has offices in Europe as well as Asia
the planned expansion will bring the total number of shops to 25

definition: if someone investigates a situation or event, they examine all the facts that they can find about it in order to decide exactly what happened, if anyone is to blame, and what action they need to take
example: specialists were called in to investigate the causes of the explosion
Sussex police are conducting a detailed investigation
Sussex police are conducting a detailed investigation

definition: a mayor is someone who has been elected to be in charge of the government of a town or city
example: the mayor and deputy mayor are elected annually by councillors
the next mayoral election will be two years from now

definition: a payment is an amount of money that you give to someone when you buy goods or services
example: our monthly mortgage payment is £900
we paid £60 for lunch

definition: a premiere is the first public performance of a play, film, TV show, etc
example: hundreds of celebrities will attend the premiere
the movie was premiered worldwide in October 2012

definition: production is the process and activity of making things in a factory. Production is also the amount of things that have been made
example: they’re moving production abroad
these products were manufactured mainly in Belgium
these products were manufactured mainly in Belgium
this company is still the major producer of glass in the state

definition: if someone or something replaces another, they start to act or be used instead of the original person or thing
example: who can replace him?
they gave him a replacement for his watch

definition: if you resign, you say officially that you are leaving your job
example: he may be forced to resign as prime minister
Farage submitted his resignation on the same day

definition: if you retweet something, you send a message on Twitter that was already sent by someone else
example: you could retweet his tweets in exchange for him retweeting yours
her retweet was not appreciated by her ex-husband

definition: a season is the months of the year when a particular activity happens. For example, the football season is the time of year when football is played; the holiday season is when most people take their holidays
example: the football season used to start in mid-September; now it starts in early August

definition: a company’s share price is the current value of a single share in the company which you can buy on the stock market
example: their share price fell by half during October

definition: if a company, especially a sports club or a music company, signs someone, they make a legal written agreement for that person to work for them. When this happens, you can also say that the player or musician signs for the club or music company
example: one of the big music companies wants to sign them
at £85m, Gareth Bale was the club’s most expensive signing

definition: to stab someone means to attack and hurt them by pushing a knife or other sharp object into them
example: he was stabbed outside the nightclub
in one incident a man suffered stab wounds
he was arrested five hours after the stabbing

definition: steel is a strong metal, made from iron and carbon
example: they’re going to close the local steel plant (factory where steel is made)

definition: a summit or a summit meeting is a meeting of the leaders of several different countries who come together to discuss important issues
example: protestors gathered outside the summit meeting

definition: when one company takes over another company, it becomes the new owner and the two companies combine to become one company
example: it was their third attempt to take over the rival company
Murdoch launched a takeover bid in early December

definition: a victim is someone who suffers as a result of a crime committed against them or as a result of an event that they cannot control
example: I’ve been a victim of three burglaries

definition: an activist is someone who does things to try to change the political or social situation, often as part of an organisation
example: a civil rights activist

definition: when people campaign, they carry out a series of actions because they want to win something, change a situation, or persuade people about something
example: we must campaign strongly to ensure that our rights are preserved
they are starting a new campaign against poverty

definition: a charity is an organisation which collects money to help people or to provide information, but which does not try to make a profit
example: he set up a charity to help poor children
she founded several charitable organisations

definition: if you consider something, you think about it carefully. If someone or something is considered to be a particular thing or to have a particular quality, then that is what lots of different people think
example: Euclid is considered to be the father of geometry

definition: if someone dominates a situation or activity, they are more successful or more active than any other people involved
example: Navratilova dominated women’s tennis for over ten years
she had a dominant personality
Taylor continued his dominance in the sport throughout 2014

definition: the founder of an institution or important organisation is the person who starts it and makes it possible for it to continue
example: Kemal Atatürk was the founder of the Turkish Republic
the RSPCA was founded in 1824

definition: a genius is someone who is extremely intelligent or clever, and much cleverer or more intelligent than almost everyone else. You can refer to someone’s great skill or ability as their genius
example: Euclid was a genius

definition: a grant is an amount of money that a government or organisation gives someone to help them do something
example: an Arts Council grant was obtained for printing the catalogue

definition: a hero is someone who does something very brave or very useful and is admired by a lot of people. Hero is usually used about men, although nowadays the word is used about women too. In the past, the word heroine was usually used about women
example: he became a national hero

definition: influence is the power to make people do what you want or to make situations develop in the way that you want. People or things that have influence are influential
example: a writer whose ideas were very influential
he has had considerable influence on British literary culture
collocations: have (an) influence on something or someone

example: he has had considerable influence on British literary culture
collocations: have (an) influence on something or someone

definition: if you inspire someone, you make them believe in something very strongly, especially in their own ability to achieve things
example: his strength and skill inspired millions around the world
an inspiring teacher

definition: a person who leads a group of people is the person who is in charge, who makes all the important decisions, and tells others what to do
example: managers must lead, motivate, and stand up for their staff
what we need is a strong leader to rebuild the company
our senior managers demonstrate strong leadership skills

definition: liberation is when a person or a country is made free after a period of being under someone else’s control
example: Atatürk led the liberation struggle in Turkey
over 100 prisoners were liberated

definition: military means relating to the army and other armed forces of a country
example: he was a military leader in the nineteenth century
he joined the military when he was just 17

definition: if you name someone or something, you choose the name that they will have. If you name someone or something after a person, you give them the same name as that person in order to remind you of the person or show respect. In American English, you name someone or something for a person
example: they named a metro station after him

definition: radiation is the form of energy that is sent out from nuclear reactions, and from substances such as uranium
example: Marie Curie studied radiation and discovered polonium
a highly radioactive substance

definition: things which are radical are extreme or involve a big change in the way something is done
example: his ideas were very radical
the situation changed radically after the election

definition: a rite is a traditional ceremony that is carried out, often for religious purposes
example: getting a tattoo is a modern rite of passage (an activity that marks a new stage in someone’s life)
the entire ritual lasted for 30 minutes

definition: a scheme is a plan or programme of action by an organisation to help people do something
example: it’s a European Union scheme to provide grants for teachers

definition: if something is a struggle, you have to try very hard to succeed because it is very difficult to achieve
example: Atatürk led the liberation struggle in Turkey
Anna has always struggled with maths

definition: to unify the different parts of a country or group means to join the separate parts together to make one single country or group
example: he was a military leader who helped unify Italy in the nineteenth century

definition: a vaccine is a kind of medicine that stops you getting a particular disease in the future
example: a vaccine against malaria
all children should be vaccinated against measles
some parents are still refusing vaccination for their children

definition: an act is something that someone does
example: it was an act of great generosity
every day we see how children have to be taught to act and behave
one person acting alone can cause a lot of damage

definition: if someone or something appears, they start to be visible. If someone appears on TV or radio, they take part in a programme that is broadcast and can be seen or heard
example: they appeared on TV regularly

definition: a bomb is a weapon that explodes and causes death and damage. Bombs can be left in public places or dropped from a plane
example: he became a hero when he removed a bomb from a bus he was driving

definition: bravery is the quality and behaviour involved in doing dangerous or difficult things without showing fear
example: it was an act of great bravery
it was an incredibly brave thing to do
they fought bravely, but without success

definition: a clip is a short film, usually a short part taken out of a longer film
example: the clip is the fourth most-watched film on YouTube

definition: a contestant is someone who competes in a game, quiz, or competition
example: a contestant on a reality TV show

definition: courage is the quality of being very brave in difficult or dangerous situations
example: Mr Singh was widely praised for his courage
Walters praised his courageous team
the crew acted courageously in helping all the passengers off the ship

definition: if something discourages you, it makes you decide not to do something
example: these problems don’t seem to discourage anyone
he gave a long and rather discouraging speech

definition: if you describe someone as elderly, you are saying they are old in a polite way. The elderly are people who are old
example: a film-maker who was interested in the experiences of the elderly
an elderly lady sat opposite me on the train

definition: something that is emotional involves someone’s feelings rather than reason and facts
example: it’s difficult to cope with the emotional stress of being famous
I was exhausted, both emotionally and physically
I was exhausted, both emotionally and physically

definition: someone’s footsteps are the sounds or marks that their feet make on the ground when they walk or run somewhere. If you follow in someone’s footsteps, you do the same things as they do and have the same success that they had
example: if you can sing, you could follow in Kurt’s footsteps
translation: шаг, след (follow in footsteps - идти по чьим-то стопам)

definition: the front page of a newspaper or magazine is the one which people see first because it is the page that faces you when you pick it up. If something is front page news, it is considered to be the most important story in the whole newspaper or magazine
example: I wouldn’t want to be on the front page of a gossip magazine

definition: generosity is willingness to give money or help people without expecting anything in return
example: an act of great generosity
she can be very generous when she wants to be
she gives generously to charity every Christmas

definition: if you go on to do something, you do it after you have done something else
example: he then went on to win the whole competition
translation: впоследствии сделать что-либо, в итоге прийти к

definition: something that is instant happens immediately, with no delay at all
example: these shows have brought instant fame to hundreds of people
I had a glass of water and instantly felt better
he woke up in an instant (immediately)

definition: if a situation is out of control, no one is able to stop or change what is happening. If someone is in control, they are able to control what is happening. If a situation is under control, it is safe, because someone is in control of it
example: before long, things start getting out of control
he was always in control and relaxed
by morning, the situation was under control

definition: if someone’s singing or a musical instrument is out of tune, they are not producing the right musical notes. If they are producing the right musical notes, then they are in tune
example: that sort of out-of-tune singing has no place on national TV
is your guitar in tune?

definition: if you praise someone or something, you say how good you think they are
example: it is a good idea to praise employees whenever possible
he was full of praise for our work

definition: if something is problematic, it is not easy to deal with because there are some problems involved
example: being a celebrity can be problematic
alcohol became a major problem in his life

definition: if you repay someone, you give back money that they gave you, or you do something to thank them for something kind that they did for you
example: he can’t afford to repay his debts
the repayment period was 25 years

definition: if one thing requires another, then it must have that other thing
example: these reality shows require no talent at all from the competitors
the minimum requirement to enter the profession is a degree

definition: your will is a legal document in which you say who should receive your money and property when you die
example: she repaid their kindness by leaving them £2m in her will

definition: a cell is the smallest part of an animal or plant that can exist on its own
example: white blood cells

definition: something that is harmful can cause harm or damage to someone or something
example: the white blood cells help destroy harmful substances
looking directly at the sun can harm your eyes
looking directly at the sun can harm your eyes

definition: a needle is a thin pointed piece of metal that you put a thread through and use for sewing things. A needle is also a thin pointed piece of metal that is pushed into someone’s body during acupuncture (a way of treating illness)
example: acupuncture involves placing needles into particular points on the body

definition: a substance is something such as wood, water, or gas that has particular qualities
example: onions contain a chemical substance that can make you cry
